title: WAR Archive
documentation:
## Purpose
WAR (Web application ARchive) is a ZIP-format archive containing a Java web application — servlets, JSPs, static resources, and a `WEB-INF/web.xml` deployment descriptor — in the structure required by Java EE / Jakarta EE servlet containers. The [WAR Archive](https://capdag.com/browse/media:war;archive) media type represents any `.war` file.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes Java web application deployments — for example, CI/CD pipelines that build and deploy WAR files to Tomcat, JBoss, WildFly, or Jetty servers. For general-purpose Java archives without a web deployment structure use [JAR](https://capdag.com/browse/media:jar;archive).

## Notes
WAR files are ZIP archives that follow a specific directory layout: `WEB-INF/web.xml` (deployment descriptor), `WEB-INF/classes/` (compiled servlets), `WEB-INF/lib/` (dependency JARs), and static content at the root. Implementers can inspect WAR contents with any ZIP tool. Servlet containers deploy WAR files by extracting them to a work directory; re-deploying requires replacing the WAR and restarting the context. The `;archive` tag indicates this is a packaged archive artifact.
